messages.content:
You are comparing two analyst responses to determine which better demonstrates knowledge of a specific finding from a longitudinal dataset.

Question asked: When did the first hepatic enzyme elevations appear relative to drug exposure initiation?

Finding to evaluate: multi-site signal distribution rules out site-specific confounders as causal explanation

Response A:
The earliest signal of a hepatic enzyme rise was recorded in **Week 9** of the trial. In the weekly operational note for the 29 April 2024 summary (Week 9) the statin‑treated subgroup showed ALT values at the upper end of the reference range, whereas the non‑statin subgroup remained stable【clinical_signal_03_ep_009】. No earlier weekly report (Weeks 1‑8) mentioned any ALT elevation, and the subsequent Week 10 note again referenced the same finding, confirming that the first detectable hepatic enzyme increase occurred in the ninth week after participants began receiving the study drug【clinical_signal_03_ep_010】.

Response B:
Hepatic enzyme elevations first appeared during the early signal phase, approximately weeks 9-10 of the trial, several weeks after subjects began receiving LNS-4821 with concomitant statin therapy.

Which response better demonstrates awareness of this finding? The response may use different terminology. Focus on whether the core insight is present, not exact wording.

Respond with ONLY one word: A, B, or TIE
/no_think
